prestazioni: LS9 6.2L Supercharged V8 swap (GM engine code: LS9, 638hp stock) with a custom-built Magnuson TVS2300 supercharger upgrade (12psi boost, 850hp) paired with a Tremec TR-6060 6-speed manual transmission conversion (Cadillac-specific bellhousing adapter from Rockland Standard Gear)

Full custom 3-inch stainless steel exhaust system by Stainless Works with 4-inch polished dual outlets, featuring 409-grade mandrel-bent tubing and Borla Pro XS mufflers (designed specifically for the Fleetwood Brougham’s frame rails and rear axle clearance)

Brembo GT 6-piston front calipers (355mm rotors) and 4-piston rear calipers (330mm rotors) with Cadallac-specific mounting brackets, paired with Ferodo DS2500 brake pads and Motul RBF 660 brake fluid for 1980s-era chassis reinforcement
estetica: Custom 1980s-inspired widebody conversion kit by D’Vinci Coachworks (hand-formed aluminum over steel, extending rear fenders by 3.5 inches and front fenders by 2.75 inches to fit 20x10 ET15 and 20x12 ET20 BBS RS-GT wheels with 275/30 and 315/25 Michelin Pilot Sport Cup 2 tires)

Full OEM-style vinyl roof delete with a custom 3M Matte Carbon Fiber wrap (applied over the original steel roof panel) and shaved drip rails, paired with 1980s Cadillac-exclusive Recaro SRD front seats (retrimmed in Alcantara with red stitching) and a custom rear bench split (60/40) with integrated 12-inch subwoofer enclosure

Retro-modern LED lighting conversion by Diode Dynamics, including sequential turn signals (OEM-style amber lenses with Philips Luxeon Z ES LEDs), Hella 500 FF fog lights (recessed into the front bumper), and a custom LED taillight assembly (using Osram Oslon Black Flat LEDs in a smoked lens housing)
